reasoning: Gibraltar is a very small, peaceful British Overseas Territory (population ~34,000) with no history of organized armed conflict in recent decades. The ACLED event type 'Violence against civilians' requires an organized armed group inflicting violence. The 360-day average prior to this window was exactly 0, meaning the benchmark to beat is 10 x (1 + 0) = 10, so 11 or more events must occur. For 11+ instances of violence against civilians by an organized armed group to randomly erupt within a 30-day period in Gibraltar—especially with no news of any such events occurring in February 2026 to date—is virtually impossible. Extensive searches revealed no ongoing conflicts, protests, or incidents of violence in the region, supporting an extremely low probability.
